title: Search shows wrong results — finding archived tasks instead of active ones
body: Something is wrong with search. When I search for tasks, I'm getting results that include old archived tasks that should be hidden, and I'm not seeing some active tasks that I know exist. For example, I searched for 'Q2 planning' and got an archived task from last year but not the active one I created yesterday. This is happening for all my searches. My teammate says she sees the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — that's definitely not the expected behavior.

To help us narrow this down: when did you first notice this? Was search working correctly before, and if so, do you recall roughly when it started showing archived results? Also, did anything change around that time — like an app update, a change to your workspace settings, or a bulk archive operation?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yes, search was working fine before. I first noticed the problem about three days ago, right after we got the v2.3.1 update. I remember because there was a maintenance window for a database migration. After that, my searches started returning wrong results. I have about 300 archived tasks and 150 active ones, and it feels like search is showing me the archived ones instead of the active ones. I haven't changed any workspace settings, and like I said, my teammate is seeing the same thing, so I don't think it's anything on my end.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That timing with the v2.3.1 update and database migration is really helpful — that's a strong lead.

One thing that would help us figure out where the problem lives: when you open that 'Q2 planning' task you created yesterday directly (not through search — maybe from your task list or a bookmark), does it show up correctly as active? And conversely, if you click on the archived task that search is surfacing, does it still show as archived when you view it?

This will help us determine whether it's the search index that's out of sync or whether the migration may have changed the underlying task data.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Good question — yes, when I open tasks directly, they show the correct status. The 'Q2 planning' task I created yesterday shows as active when I view it from my task list, and the old archived one from last year still shows as archived when I click into it. So the tasks themselves seem fine, it's just that search is pulling up the wrong ones. It's like the search filter is flipped — when I search with the default 'active tasks only' filter, I get archived tasks, and if I switch the filter to show archived tasks, I actually see my active ones. Really confusing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search index status flag inverted after v2.3.1 database migration — active/archived filter returns opposite results","problem":"After the v2.3.1 update and associated database migration, the search index returns archived tasks when the 'active tasks only' filter is selected, and active tasks when the 'archived tasks' filter is selected. The underlying task data is correct — tasks viewed directly show the right status. The issue affects all searches for all users in the workspace.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3.1 database migration likely inverted the status flag (or equivalent boolean/enum) in the search index. The source-of-truth task records were not affected, but the denormalized status value written to the search index during migration has active and archived swapped. This could be a literal boolean inversion (e.g., `is_archived` written as `!is_archived`), a mapping error in an enum conversion, or the migration re-indexed with a filter predicate that was accidentally negated.","reproduction_steps":["Log in to a workspace that went through the v2.3.1 database migration","Create or identify a task with 'active' status and a known keyword (e.g., 'Q2 planning')","Search for that keyword using the default 'active tasks only' filter","Observe that archived tasks matching the keyword appear, but the active task does not","Switch the search filter to 'archived tasks'","Observe that the active task now appears in results"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1, post-database-migration. Workspace with ~300 archived and ~150 active tasks. Confirmed on at least two user accounts.","severity":"high","impact":"All users in affected workspaces cannot use search effectively — every search returns inverted results. Workaround exists (manually select the opposite filter), but this is confusing and unreliable for users who don't realize the inversion. Core search functionality is broken.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the v2.3.1 migration script for how task status was written to the search index — look for a boolean inversion or negated predicate. 2. Check the search index entries directly (e.g., query Elasticsearch/equivalent) to confirm the status field is inverted relative to the source database. 3. Fix the migration logic and re-index all tasks from the source-of-truth data. 4. Verify that the search service's filter query correctly maps 'active' filter to the non-archived status value (in case the inversion is in the query layer rather than the index).","proposed_test_case":"After re-indexing: search for a known active task with the 'active tasks only' filter and confirm it appears; search with the 'archived' filter and confirm it does not. Repeat for a known archived task (should appear under archived filter, not active). Run across a dataset with mixed statuses to confirm no tasks leak across filters.","information_gaps":["Which search backend is used (Elasticsearch, database full-text, etc.) — determines exact fix approach","Whether the issue affects all workspaces or only those that went through migration during the specific maintenance window","Exact migration script that ran during v2.3.1 — not yet inspected"]}
